title: Slash commands: /lux on, /lux off, /lux
CLI Layer 1: notify, theme, status commands
Custom status bar with debug and production modes
description: Replace HelloImGui default status bar (Enable idling + FPS) with a custom one. Debug mode: scene ID, client count, element count, FPS. Production mode: minimal or hidden. Reference: runner_params.callbacks.show_status callback.

title: Built-in menu item for font size adjustment
description: Replace the version number in the Lux menu dropdown with Increase Font Size / Decrease Font Size controls. Also persist user preferences (font size and selected theme) across sessions via the INI file. Currently neither setting survives a restart — hello_imgui.apply_theme() is fire-and-forget and there's no font size control at all. This is a display-server-side feature — no MCP round-trip needed.

title: Phase 1: Walking Skeleton — End-to-End Slice
description: The single most critical user journey: Claude calls lux.show() → display renders image + buttons → user clicks → event returns to Claude. Touches every layer. Riskiest integrations first.

title: Display protocol module: message types, serialization, framing
description: Port and refine spikes/c-protocol-design/protocol.py into src/punt_lux/protocol.py. Dataclasses for all 9 message types, 4 element types. encode_frame/decode_frame. FrameReader for non-blocking socket reads. Full type annotations. 100% unit test coverage.

title: Display server: ImGui window with socket listener and basic renderer
description: Create src/punt_lux/display.py. ImGui window via imgui-bundle (immapp.run). Listens on Unix socket. FrameReader drains socket via select() per frame. Renders basic scene (text + image + button). Sends ack and interaction events. Entry point: lux display. Texture cache for image elements.
close_reason: Display server implemented with ImGui render loop, Unix socket IPC, texture cache, Z spec, and unit tests. PR #6 merged + follow-up fixes pushed.

title: Client library: connect, send scene, receive events
description: Create src/punt_lux/client.py. LuxClient class: connect to display socket, send scene/update/clear/ping messages, receive ready/ack/interaction/window events. Auto-launch display if not running. Blocking and non-blocking receive modes.

title: MCP server: lux.show, lux.update, lux.clear tools
description: Create src/punt_lux/server.py. FastMCP server with tools: show (sends scene, waits for ack), update (sends patches), clear, ping. Tool descriptions optimized for LLM understanding. stdio transport. Returns interaction events as tool results.

title: CLI entry points: lux display, lux version, lux status
description: Create src/punt_lux/cli.py with typer. Commands: display (starts the display server), version, status (checks if display is running, reports socket path). Register as console_scripts entry point in pyproject.toml.

title: Walking skeleton E2E test: show image + buttons, click, receive event
description: E2E test that: starts display server in subprocess, connects client, sends scene with image + 2 buttons, verifies scene renders (via ack), simulates button click (or uses a test-mode flag), verifies interaction event returns. This is the proof that the full pipeline works.

title: Integration test: socket IPC round-trip under load
description: Port and refine spikes/b-ipc-render/stress_test.py into tests/. Send 100 rapid scene updates, verify zero drops, measure RTT. Test graceful disconnection. Test reconnection after display restart.

title: Table auto-sizing: proportional column widths from content
Phase 4: Code-on-Demand
description: The escape hatch. Consent dialog, CodeExecutor with RenderContext, AST warning scanner, hot-reload. Follows same permission model as Claude Code Bash.
close_reason: All 4 children complete. Code-on-demand fully operational: consent dialog, AST scanner, code executor, render_function element kind.

title: Consent dialog: ImGui modal for code review and approval
description: Port spikes/e-code-on-demand/consent.py into src/punt_lux/consent.py. Modal window with code display, AST warnings, Allow/Deny buttons. Integrates into the display server's render loop.

title: Code executor: RenderContext, compile, per-frame call
description: Port spikes/e-code-on-demand/runtime.py into src/punt_lux/runtime.py. CodeExecutor class: compile source, extract render(ctx), call each frame. RenderContext with state dict, dt, frame counter, dimensions, event sender. Error handling and hot-reload.

title: AST warning scanner
description: Port spikes/e-code-on-demand/ast_check.py into src/punt_lux/ast_check.py. Flags suspicious imports, builtins, attribute access. Returns warnings for consent dialog. Unit tests.

title: render_function element kind: wire consent + executor into renderer
description: Add render_function element kind to the dispatch table. When received: show consent dialog. On allow: create CodeExecutor, call render(ctx) each frame. On deny: show denied message. Wire ctx.send() to the event queue. Integration test.
close_reason: render_function element wired into display server. Consent→executor→event routing verified end-to-end. Hot reload preserves ctx.state. DES-012 written.

title: Spike C: Display protocol design — message format and flow specification
close_reason: All five spikes complete and verified

title: Built-in table detail panel: row selection + split layout
close_reason: Built-in table detail panel with row selection, scrollable detail body, 2-column field grid, DES-019 ADR

title: Spike A: imgui-bundle viability — window, image, buttons, clicks
close_reason: All five spikes complete and verified

title: Fold set_theme into show() as a parameter — eliminate round trip
description: set_theme is currently a separate MCP tool call. Every skill that sets a theme before showing a scene requires two round trips (set_theme + show). Instead, add an optional 'theme' parameter to show() so it can be done in one call. Keep set_theme as a standalone tool for cases where you want to change theme without replacing the scene, but make the common path (theme + show) a single call.

title: Built-in table keyboard navigation (up/down arrow)
description: When a table row is selected and detail is present, up/down arrow keys should move selection to the previous/next row. Display server handles this in the render loop — no recv/update needed. Extends DES-019 selectable rows.

title: Phase 0: Test Infrastructure & Project Scaffolding
description: Risk register, pyproject.toml, src/punt_lux package, tests/, CI pipeline, quality gates passing. The harness before any feature code.

title: Risk register: identify riskiest integration points
description: Before writing code, document: imgui-bundle cross-platform rendering, Unix socket IPC reliability, MCP stdio transport integration, OpenGL texture lifecycle, ImPlot initialization requirements. Output: RISKS.md

title: Project scaffolding: pyproject.toml, src/punt_lux/, tests/
description: Create pyproject.toml with punt-lux package, ruff/mypy/pyright config, pytest setup. Create src/punt_lux/__init__.py with version. Create tests/ with conftest.py. Follow punt-kit Python standards exactly. Use uv as package manager.

title: Test harness: pytest fixtures, unit/integration/e2e scaffold
description: Set up pytest with markers for unit/integration/e2e. Create test fixtures for: mock display server, mock socket, sample scene data. Create one passing test at each level (trivial). Coverage reporting with pytest-cov.

title: CI pipeline: GitHub Actions workflow
description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml running quality gates: ruff check, ruff format --check, mypy, pyright, pytest. Matrix: Python 3.12, 3.13. Follow punt-kit GitHub standards.
close_reason: CI already exists and passing: lint.yml (ruff, mypy, pyright), test.yml (pytest), docs.yml (markdownlint). All green on PRs #1 and #2.

title: Quality gates green: all linters and tests passing
description: Verify the full quality gate command passes: uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check . && uv run mypy src/ tests/ && uv run pyright && uv run pytest. Zero errors, zero warnings.
close_reason: All quality gates verified green: ruff, mypy, pyright, pytest, markdownlint. make check passes.

title: Acceptance criteria format: define test patterns for each phase
description: Document how acceptance is measured: functional (what system must do), performance (latency bounds for IPC, rendering), error (failure modes handled gracefully). Write to tests/ACCEPTANCE.md.

title: Skill: status board (kanban / roadmap view)
description:
Skill: /lux:status-board — kanban / roadmap columns.

Delivery mechanism (Option D hybrid):
- Skill prompt walks the LLM through: define columns (e.g. Todo/In Progress/Done) → populate cards per column (text + badges in groups) → show() with columns layout → recv() loop for card click/drag events → update() to move cards between columns.
- Template example embedded in skill prompt: a 3-column kanban with 2-3 cards each, showing title, assignee, and priority badge.
- MCP instructions primed for formatting rules.

Used by: prfaq section tracker (draft/review/approved columns), beads ready/in-progress/done view, project roadmap visualization. Natural fit for any workflow with stages.

title: Phase 8: User Commands & Hook Architecture
Phase 7: Lux Skills — predefined patterns for common visual intents
description: Create a library of Lux skills/templates that help LLMs convert intent into good visual patterns. Skills codify best practices so agents don't reinvent layouts each time. Examples: presentation (slides+narration), dashboard (metrics+charts), data explorer (table+filters), architecture diagram, status board, kanban. Each skill is a prompt template that knows which elements to compose and how to lay them out.

title: Skill: beads board — list/detail view for beads issues
description: Build a /lux:beads skill that displays project beads in the Lux list/detail table pattern. LLM reads beads data via bd list/bd show, populates a table with filters (status, priority, type) and detail panel with full description. Ships as a default out-of-box use case demonstrating the built-in table features (DES-018 filters + DES-019 detail).

title: imgui_md does not render inside begin_child() regions
description: imgui_md.render_unindented() produces no output inside imgui.begin_child() regions, printing 'ImGuiMd::Render : Markdown was not initialized\!' to stderr at 60fps. The same call works fine outside begin_child(). Calling imgui_md.initialize_markdown() at the render site does not fix it. This blocks markdown rendering in the table detail panel body. Current workaround: use imgui.text_wrapped() instead.

title: Add specialized show tools (show_table, show_diagram, show_dashboard)
description:
Thin wrappers around show() that provide focused docstrings for common patterns. Each tool constructs the element tree and delegates to the existing show() code path — no new rendering logic.

Motivation: The generic show() docstring tries to teach 15+ element types at once. AIs without a skill loaded cannot discover advanced features like table filters and detail panels. Specialized tools give each pattern a dedicated docstring with complete examples.

Tools to add:
- show_table(scene_id, columns, rows, filters?, detail?, flags?, title?) — list/detail explorer with built-in filtering
- show_diagram(scene_id, ...) — node-edge graph visualization (params TBD, may need renderer work)
- show_dashboard(scene_id, ...) — metrics cards + charts + tables (params TBD)

Design: Start thin (params mirror element schema), thicken based on where AIs stumble. Generic show() remains for freeform composition.
close_reason: Added show_table and show_dashboard tools — thin wrappers with focused docstrings

title: Extend ImGui font atlas to cover common Unicode glyphs
close_reason: Implemented cross-platform font loading: Arial Unicode (primary) + Apple Symbols (merge) on macOS, DejaVu Sans + Noto Sans Symbols2 on Linux. All geometric shapes, arrows, box drawing, Z notation, and double-struck letters render on macOS. Linux verification tracked in lux-zn2.

title: Phase 3: Data & Visualization
description: Tables, ImPlot integration (line, scatter, bar, heatmap), progress bars, spinners, markdown rendering via imgui_md.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete: TableElement, PlotElement, ProgressElement, SpinnerElement, MarkdownElement, tooltip support on all elements

title: Table element: rows, columns, headers, sortable
description: Add table element kind using imgui.begin_table API. Columns with labels, types, widths. Rows as arrays. Optional sorting. Port from spike D. Unit tests.
close_reason: Merged PR #16: TableElement with columns, rows, flags, ImGui renderer, full test coverage

title: Plot element: ImPlot integration (line, scatter, bar, heatmap)
description: Add plot element kind wrapping ImPlot. Initialize ImPlot at display startup. Support line, scatter, bar, heatmap plot types. Data as arrays. Port from spike D. Unit tests.
close_reason: PlotElement implemented: dataclass, serializer/deserializer, ImPlot addon init, renderer, tests (6 new), DESIGN.md decisions (DES-010, DES-011), demo (demos/data_viz.py)

title: Progress, spinner, and markdown elements
description: Add progress (imgui.progress_bar), spinner (imspinner), and markdown (imgui_md.render_unindented) element kinds. Unit tests.
close_reason: ProgressElement, SpinnerElement, MarkdownElement: dataclasses, serializers, deserializers, renderers, tests

title: Tooltip support on all elements
description: Add optional tooltip field to all element kinds. Render on hover via imgui.set_item_tooltip(). Low effort, high value for rich information display.
close_reason: tooltip field added to all 21 element dataclasses, serialized/deserialized centrally in _element_to_dict/element_from_dict, rendered via imgui.set_item_tooltip in _render_element

title: Built-in table pagination (client-side)
description: When a table has more rows than fit in a fixed-height list region, paginate client-side. Display N rows per page with Previous/Next controls. The table list portion gets a fixed max height; the detail panel takes remaining space below. Extends DES-018/DES-019 built-in table features.

title: Skill: architecture diagram (boxes + arrows + labels)
description:
Skill: /lux:architecture — boxes + arrows + labels for system diagrams.

Delivery mechanism (Option D hybrid):
- Skill prompt walks the LLM through: identify components → define positions and connections → compose draw canvas with rect commands for boxes, line/bezier commands for arrows, text commands for labels → show() → optional recv() for click-to-inspect on components.
- Template example embedded in skill prompt: a 3-component architecture diagram (client → server → database) with labeled arrows.
- MCP instructions primed for formatting rules.

Used by: z-spec schema hierarchy visualization, quarry architecture overview, project dependency graphs. The draw canvas element already supports rect, line, bezier, text, circle, triangle, and polyline — sufficient for most architecture diagrams.

title: Phase 2: Scene Vocabulary — Full Element Dispatch
description: Expand beyond the skeleton's basic elements. Full interactive vocabulary (slider, checkbox, combo, input_text, radio, color_picker), draw commands (ImDrawList), layout system (groups, tabs, collapsing headers), state management.

title: Interactive elements: slider, checkbox, combo, input_text, radio, color_picker
description: Add interactive element kinds to the renderer dispatch table. Each generates interaction events on value change. Port from spikes/d-json-vocabulary/renderer.py. WidgetState management for persistent values. Unit tests for each element kind.

title: Draw commands: ImDrawList primitives as JSON
description: Add draw element kind with sub-commands: line, rect, circle, triangle, bezier, text, polyline. Coordinate transformation from local canvas to screen. Port from spike D. Unit tests.

title: Layout system: group, tab_bar, collapsing_header
description: Add layout container elements. group with layout: rows|columns|grid. tab_bar with tab items. collapsing_header with children. Recursive rendering support. Unit tests.
close_reason: Implemented GroupElement, TabBarElement, CollapsingHeaderElement, WindowElement with recursive rendering, serialization, and tests. Demos in demos/

title: Selectable and list elements
description: Add selectable (clickable list items) and tree (recursive collapsible nodes) element kinds. Unit tests.

title: Skill architecture: generic templates + project customization
description:
Architecture decision: Hybrid skill + preset approach (Option D).

Lux provides reusable UI solutions to LLMs through three complementary delivery mechanisms:

1. **MCP instructions field** (Prior-Context Priming) — General formatting and behavioral rules delivered at session start. Lives in system context before any tool call. Most reliable for rules the LLM must always follow.

2. **Skill prompts** (.md files in the plugin) — Multi-phase workflows that walk the LLM through composing a visual pattern. Each skill is a recipe: gather data → compose elements from a template → show() → set up recv() loop for interactions. Skills encode intent and best practices, not rigid layouts.

3. **Tool descriptions with pattern examples** — The show() docstring already serves as an element reference card. Enrich it with complete layout examples (not just element syntax). The LLM starts from a known-good structure and adapts.

Generic Lux skills ship as starting points:
- /lux:dashboard — metrics cards + charts + status table
- /lux:presentation — slides + narration (Vox integration)
- /lux:data-explorer — table + filters + detail view
- /lux:status-board — kanban / roadmap columns
- /lux:architecture — boxes + arrows + labels

Projects compose and customize into domain-specific layouts:
- dungeon → Wizardry HUD (claude-dungeon-rth)
- z-spec → interactive state explorer (claude-z-spec-plugin-7bf)
- prfaq → meeting decision dashboard (prfaq-y0s)
- quarry → search results UI (quarry-f0t)
- langlearn-tts → lesson dashboard + flashcards (lltts-q0m)

Each project creates its own skill that imports the Lux pattern but adapts the layout. The skill teaches the pattern; the tools execute it. This follows punt-kit's Extension Point Selection guide: skills for complex multi-phase workflows, MCP tools for deterministic operations.

title: Skill: presentation (slides + narration)
description:
Skill: /lux:presentation — slides + narration with Vox integration.

Delivery mechanism (Option D hybrid):
- Skill prompt walks the LLM through: define slide content → compose elements (markdown + image per slide, navigation buttons) → show() → recv() loop for Next/Back button clicks → update() to swap slide content → unmute() via Vox for narration at each step.
- Template example embedded in skill prompt: a complete 3-slide presentation JSON tree the LLM adapts.
- MCP instructions primed at session start for formatting rules.

Already prototyped: the Lux+Vox presentation demo proved this works with zero new code — agent orchestrates show()/update() for visuals and unmute() for narration. The skill codifies that pattern so any agent can do it without reinventing the composition.

title: Skill: data explorer (table + filters + detail view)
description:
Skill: /lux:data-explorer — table + filters + detail view.

Delivery mechanism (Option D hybrid):
- Skill prompt walks the LLM through: identify data source → compose filter controls (combos, checkboxes, text input) → compose data table → show() with split layout (filters sidebar + main table) → recv() loop for filter changes and row selection → update() table when filters change → show detail panel on row select.
- Template example embedded in skill prompt: a complete data-explorer JSON tree with 3 filter controls, a sortable table, and a detail collapsing header.
- MCP instructions primed for formatting rules.

This is the most reusable pattern — quarry search results, z-spec partition tables, biff session history, and prfaq FAQ lists all follow this shape. Domain projects customize the filter types, column definitions, and detail view content.

title: Skill: dashboard (metrics + charts + status)
description:
Skill: /lux:dashboard — metrics cards + charts + status indicators.

Delivery mechanism (Option D hybrid):
- Skill prompt walks the LLM through: gather metric values → compose header row (text elements with key numbers) → compose chart area (plot elements) → compose status table → show() with multi-window or group layout → optional recv() loop for refresh button or auto-refresh interval.
- Template example embedded in skill prompt: a complete dashboard JSON tree with 4 metric cards in a columns group, a line chart, and a status table below.
- MCP instructions primed for formatting rules.

Used by: prfaq meeting dashboard (hot-spot map + persona cards + risk matrix), langlearn lesson progress (mastery bars + vocabulary table), quarry collection overview (document counts + search frequency chart).

title: Phase 5: Image Generation Backends
description: OpenAI as first backend. Provider abstraction layer. Multi-provider support following Vox pattern. Image caching.

title: Provider abstraction: ImageProvider protocol and registry
description: Create src/punt_lux/providers/base.py with ImageProvider protocol (generate method). Registry for looking up providers by name. Follow Vox pattern. Unit tests.

title: OpenAI image generation backend
description: Create src/punt_lux/providers/openai.py. Implements ImageProvider using OpenAI API (gpt-image-1 or dall-e-3). Handles API key from environment. Saves generated images to temp directory. Returns file path for display protocol. Integration test with real API.

title: MCP tool: lux.generate — generate image and display it
description: Add generate tool to MCP server. Takes prompt, optional provider, optional size. Calls provider, saves image, sends scene to display with image + Regenerate/Accept buttons. Returns interaction result (which button user clicked).

title: Spike B: IPC + render loop — Unix socket feeding ImGui bidirectionally
close_reason: All five spikes complete and verified

title: Phase 3.5: Menu Bar — window chrome with Size, Theme, Quit
close_reason: Merged PR #17: built-in Lux/Theme/Window menus + agent-extensible custom menus

title: PostToolUse Bash hook: signal accumulation classifier
Stop hook: decision-block with re-entry guard
Built-in table filtering: search and combo filters at render time
description: DES-018: Add optional 'filters' field to TableElement. Display server renders filter controls above the table and applies them at 60fps — no recv/update round trips. Scope: protocol addition (TableFilter dataclass), display.py renderer changes (_render_table adds filter controls + row filtering), auto result count line. Does NOT include sort-by-column (future extension). See DESIGN.md DES-018 for full spec.

title: Display process name and app icon
description: The lux display process shows as 'Python 3.13' in the macOS dock/task switcher and uses a blank document icon. Fix both: (1) Set the process name to 'Lux' so it appears correctly in Activity Monitor, dock, and window switcher. (2) Set a proper application icon (not the default Python/blank paper icon). May require an Info.plist bundle or platform-specific APIs (NSApplication on macOS, WM hints on Linux).

title: Spike E: Code-on-demand with consent — exec render functions in ImGui loop with permission gate
close_reason: All five spikes complete and verified

title: Spike D: Extended JSON vocabulary — map ImGui primitives, build a diagram + interactive demo
close_reason: All five spikes complete and verified

title: CLI Layer 3: hook dispatchers (stop, post-bash, session-start)
Theme control: set_theme MCP tool and show() theme hint
Config state: .lux/config.md and signals.json
Phase 6: Polish & Distribution
description: Always-on-top, window position persistence, install.sh, plugin shell packaging, error UX, performance optimization, accessibility.

title: Always-on-top and window position persistence
description: Set SDL/GLFW window hint for always-on-top (toggleable). Save window position and size to ~/.lux/window.json on close. Restore on next launch.

title: install.sh and PyPI packaging
description: Create install.sh following punt-kit pattern. Verify pyproject.toml has correct entry points, dependencies, classifiers. Test pip install from local source. Test uv tool install.

title: Plugin shell packaging for Claude Code marketplace
description: Create .claude-plugin/ directory with plugin.json, MCP server registration, install hook. Follow punt-kit plugin standards and distribution standards.

title: Error UX: graceful degradation for all failure modes
description: Handle: display not running (auto-launch or clear error), socket disconnect mid-scene, invalid scene JSON, texture load failure, ImPlot not initialized. Each failure mode should produce a clear user-visible message, not a crash.

title: Verify Unicode font loading on Linux
description: Verify that the cross-platform font loading in display.py works on Linux. On macOS, Arial Unicode + Apple Symbols (merged) renders all geometric shapes, arrows, box drawing, Z notation symbols, and double-struck letters. Linux path candidates: DejaVuSans.ttf (primary) + NotoSansSymbols2-Regular.ttf (merge). Test: run the font glyph test scene and confirm all rows render. If DejaVu is missing symbols that Arial Unicode has, may need to add additional merge fonts or adjust the candidate list.
notes: Verified on Ubuntu 24.04: DejaVuSans.ttf at /usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/, NotoSansSymbols2-Regular.ttf at /usr/share/fonts/truetype/noto/. lux doctor confirms both. Font loading paths work as designed.
